normalize: P1: No design tokens for spacing — padding values are ad-hoc (48px, 36px, 32px, 24px) with no consistent scale
P1: Inconsistent icon system — uses raw Unicode geometric shapes (⬡ ◈ △ ◇ ▽ ○) instead of a proper icon set; rendering varies across OSes
P2: No component reuse pattern — .pipeline-step, .feature, .agent-card are structurally identical but styled separately
P2: CSS custom properties exist but spacing/sizing aren't tokenized, only colors
harden: P0: 'Documentation' and 'Changelog' links point to '#' — dead links with no indication they're non-functional
P1: No 404 or error page handling
P1: No loading states or skeleton screens — content just pops in via reveal animation
P2: No form validation or input handling present (no interactive elements beyond links)
P2: External Google Fonts dependency — if CDN fails, fallback fonts will cause layout shift
optimize: P1: All CSS is inlined in a single 685-line HTML file — no code splitting, no caching benefit for styles
P1: Noise texture SVG filter is applied as a full-viewport fixed overlay — GPU compositing cost on every frame during scroll
P1: Blueprint grid background uses 4 layered gradients on a fixed-position element — unnecessary paint cost
P2: Google Fonts loads 3 font families (6+ weights) — heavy download for a landing page (~150-200KB)
P2: No image optimization concerns (no images used), but also no favicon or OG metadata
P3: IntersectionObserver threshold of 0.1 is fine but elements never un-observe after becoming visible
adapt: P0: At 900px breakpoint, pipeline collapses to single column but arrow connectors ('→') just hide — no visual flow indicator replacement
P1: No tablet breakpoint — jumps from desktop (3-col) straight to mobile (1-col) at 900px
P1: Hero uses clamp() for font sizing but body text remains fixed at 18px/16px/13px — poor scaling on very large or very small screens
P2: No dark/light mode toggle — assumes dark preference with no system preference detection
P2: Footer flex layout will break awkwardly between 600-900px range
clarify: P1: Hero subtitle text is dense — 'multi-agent code review, architecture decision records, and compounding knowledge' is jargon-heavy for a landing page
P1: No clear CTA button — the page describes features but never tells users what to DO (no 'Get Started' button, no install link)
P2: Section labels (§ 01, § 02) use typographic conventions most users won't recognize
P2: Agent card descriptions are terse to the point of being unclear ('Bottlenecks, complexity, query optimization')
P2: '/re:feature' command syntax is not explained — unfamiliar to anyone who hasn't used the plugin

findings.description: No primary CTA exists anywhere on the page. The hero section describes the product but offers no actionable button, install link, or sign-up. Users scroll through 4000px of content with no conversion point.
findings.recommendation: Add a prominent CTA button in the hero ('Get Started' or 'Install Plugin') and repeat it after the code section. Use contrasting fill color (--blue) with sufficient padding.

findings.description: Documentation and Changelog footer links are dead (#). Users who click them get no feedback and stay on the same page — violating user trust and system status visibility.
findings.recommendation: Either link to real destinations or remove/disable them with a 'Coming Soon' tooltip. Never ship dead links.

findings.description: The full-page screenshot reveals that ~70% of the viewport on initial load is empty dark space. Below-fold content is invisible because .reveal elements start at opacity:0 and only animate on scroll. If JS fails, ALL content below the hero is permanently invisible.
findings.recommendation: Add a CSS-only fallback (.no-js .reveal { opacity: 1; transform: none; }) and reduce hero min-height from 100vh to allow a content teaser above the fold.

findings.description: Body text at 13px (pipeline step descriptions, agent descriptions) is below WCAG recommended minimum. The --text-dim color (#5a6a7e) on --bg (#0a0e17) has a contrast ratio of approximately 3.2:1, failing WCAG AA (requires 4.5:1).
findings.recommendation: Increase minimum body text to 14px. Lighten --text-dim to at least #7a8a9e to meet AA contrast. Test with WebAIM contrast checker.

findings.description: The --text-dim (#5a6a7e) color used for section labels, step numbers, mono text, and agent types fails WCAG AA contrast against the dark background. This affects roughly 40% of all visible text on the page.
findings.recommendation: Establish a minimum contrast of 4.5:1 for all text. Adjust --text-dim to #8494a7 or similar. Audit every text/background combination.

findings.description: Pipeline grid uses 5 equal columns with arrow connectors. At medium viewport widths (901-1100px), each column becomes very narrow (~180px), making text cramped and arrows overlap with content.
findings.recommendation: Add a breakpoint at ~1100px to switch to a 3+2 or scrollable horizontal layout. Consider a vertical timeline layout for narrower screens.

findings.description: No navigation or header bar. On a 4000px-tall page, users have no way to jump between sections. The only navigation is 3 footer links (2 of which are dead).
findings.recommendation: Add a sticky top nav with section anchors (Workflow, Capabilities, Agents, Get Started). Use smooth scroll with active state indicators.

findings.description: The blueprint/engineering grid aesthetic is visually coherent but follows a well-worn dark-mode developer landing page template. The grid overlay, noise texture, and geometric Unicode icons are generic patterns seen on dozens of dev tool sites.
findings.recommendation: Introduce one distinctive visual element — custom illustrations, animated diagrams of the pipeline flow, or interactive demos. The content is unique; the presentation should match.

findings.description: No favicon, no meta description, no Open Graph tags, no Twitter card metadata. Sharing this URL on any platform will produce a blank preview card.
findings.recommendation: Add meta description, og:title, og:description, og:image, and twitter:card meta tags. Create a favicon that matches the blueprint aesthetic.

findings.description: Zero ARIA roles, landmarks, or labels on the entire page. Screen readers will see a flat document with no semantic navigation. Interactive elements (hover states on cards) have no keyboard or assistive technology equivalents.
findings.recommendation: Add role='navigation' to footer links, role='main' to <main>, aria-label to sections. Ensure all hover-interactive elements are keyboard focusable.

findings.description: Agent grid only shows 8 of the claimed 29 agents (27%) with no indication more exist. Users may think the list is complete.
findings.recommendation: Add a 'View all 29 agents →' expandable section or link. Showing a subset without context undermines credibility.

findings.description: Three font families loaded (Instrument Serif, Schibsted Grotesk, DM Mono) is excessive. The serif display font is only used for headings, creating a heavy download for minimal typographic variety.
findings.recommendation: Consider dropping to 2 families. Subset fonts for only needed characters. Use font-display: swap (already partially in place via display=swap param).

findings.description: Scroll-reveal animations are one-directional — elements animate in but never out. Scrolling up shows static content, creating an inconsistent experience. Animation timing (0.6s ease) is uniform across all elements, feeling mechanical.
findings.recommendation: Vary animation timing slightly. Consider bidirectional reveals or at minimum, ensure the page feels intentional on upward scroll.

findings.description: Color palette is monochromatic blue with only two accent colors (cyan for code, orange for commands). The feature cards, agent cards, and pipeline steps all look nearly identical — no visual hierarchy through color differentiation.
findings.recommendation: Use subtle color coding to differentiate sections or card types. Agent types (Review, Research, Design, Workflow) should each have a distinct accent color.

findings.description: Code block section ('Three Commands to Ship') has no copy-to-clipboard functionality. Developer landing pages universally support this — its absence feels unfinished.
findings.recommendation: Add a small copy button on each code block. Use a brief 'Copied!' tooltip on click.

findings.description: No search, no skip-to-content link, no keyboard shortcuts. Power users have no accelerators.
findings.recommendation: Add skip-to-content link, consider Cmd+K search for documentation content.

summary: A competent dark-mode developer landing page with strong typographic hierarchy and a coherent blueprint aesthetic, but critically lacking in conversion design (no CTA), accessibility (zero ARIA, failing contrast ratios), and interactivity (dead links, no navigation, no copy buttons). The content architecture is clear and the information hierarchy is logical, but the presentation feels like an unfinished template — visually consistent yet generic, technically sound in structure but missing production-ready polish. The page communicates what the product does but fails to help users take the next step. Score of 58 reflects a solid foundation that needs significant work on accessibility, UX affordances, and distinctive personality before it's ready for public use.
